title: Kim
description: Kim — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: 'A knowledge base from Rudyard Kipling''s novel Kim'

memes.label: "Chapter I"
memes.description: 'Kim meets a Tibetan lama at the Lahore Museum and becomes his chela, beginning their journey together.'

relations.label: Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: "Chapter X"
memes.description: 'Kim reunites with Mahbub Ali and receives new instructions in the Great Game.'

memes.label: "Chapter XI"
memes.description: 'Kim and the lama travel with the widow of Kulu, encountering new challenges and allies.'

memes.label: "Chapter XII"
memes.description: 'Kim deepens his involvement in the Great Game while caring for the aging lama.'

relations.label: Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: "Chapter XIII"
memes.description: 'Kim confronts the Russian and French spies, securing important documents for the British.'

memes.label: "Chapter XIV"
memes.description: 'Kim completes his mission in the Great Game but struggles with his identity between two worlds.'

memes.label: "Chapter XV"
memes.description: 'The lama finds his River of the Arrow and achieves enlightenment; Kim''s destiny is affirmed.'

memes.label: "Chapter II"
memes.description: 'Kim and the lama travel by train to Umballa, where Kim delivers a secret message for Mahbub Ali.'

relations.label: Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: "Chapter III"
memes.description: 'The lama and Kim continue their journey, encountering various characters along the Grand Trunk Road.'

relations.label: Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: "Chapter IV"
memes.description: 'Kim meets the Maverick regiment, is recognized as Kimball O''Hara''s son, and is sent to school.'

memes.label: "Chapter V"
memes.description: 'Kim begins his education at St. Xavier''s School while staying with Lurgan Sahib during holidays.'

relations.label: Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: "Chapter VI"
memes.description: 'Kim trains in espionage under Lurgan Sahib and learns the Great Game from Colonel Creighton.'

memes.label: "Chapter VII"
memes.description: 'Kim rejoins the lama and travels with him, meeting a Russian and French spy duo along the way.'

memes.label: "Chapter VIII"
memes.description: 'Kim and the lama travel through the Himalayas, and Kim foils the foreign spies'' plans.'

memes.label: "Chapter IX"
memes.description: 'Kim continues his journey with the lama through the mountains, growing in wisdom and skill.'

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Abbottabad
memes.description: 'A British garrison town in the Hazara region (present-day Pakistan), mentioned in Kim as an entry point to the Himalayas via the short road from Srinagar. (013-024)'

relations.label: The Russian Spies Enter the Hills

memes.label: Abdullah
memes.description: 'The sweetmeat-seller''s son, a Mohammedan boy who plays on Zam-Zammah with Kim and Chota Lal in the Lahore bazar. (001-008)'

memes.label: "Agra"
memes.description: 'A major city on the Grand Trunk Road where the spy E23 is recognized and nearly captured during his flight in the Great Game.'

memes.label: Akrola
memes.description: 'A village on the plains route, known as "Akrola of the Ford," situated along the Grand Trunk Road between Aminabad and Sahaigunge. (012-050)'

memes.label: "Amritzar"
memes.description: 'The holy city of the Sikhs in Punjab, referenced as the birthplace of Kim''s father and a waypoint on the journey from Lahore.'

memes.label: Ao-chung
memes.description: 'A settlement in the Himalayan region whose men serve as shikarris (trackers and hunting guides) for British sahibs including Yankling Sahib and Fostum Sahib. (013-085)'

memes.label: Badrinath
memes.description: 'A towering Himalayan peak, paired with Kedarnath as the "kings of that wilderness," visible on the northern route through the mountains. (013-009)'

memes.label: "Bandakui"
memes.description: 'A small railway town in Rajasthan where one of the Great Game agents resides, serving as a safe point on E23''s escape route.'

memes.label: "Benares"
memes.description: 'The ancient holy city on the Ganges, the lama''s stated pilgrimage destination and a recurring spiritual lodestar throughout the novel.'

memes.label: Bhumia
memes.description: 'A Hindu village deity, the God of the Homestead, to whom the Jat cultivator gives thanks for the fertility of his land. (002-097)'

memes.label: Bikanir
memes.description: 'A mysterious desert city with wells four hundred feet deep, visited by Kim and Mahbub Ali during a camel expedition where Kim maps the city''s fortifications (Chapter 10).'

memes.label: Bombay
memes.description: 'The major port city on India''''s western coast, described in the novel as "the queen of all" cities, serving as a commercial hub for Mahbub Ali''''s horse-trading and a gateway for Kim''''s travels.'

memes.label: "Buddh Gaya"
memes.description: "The site of the Mahabodhi Temple, a major Buddhist pilgrimage destination in Bihar where the lama intends to travel and where the Kulu woman goes to make shraddha for her late husband. (001-062)"

memes.label: Bunár
memes.description: 'A hill state ruled by a Rajah with guns, paired with Hilás as a destination for Russian agents and a node in the Great Game''s Himalayan frontier politics. (012-145)'

memes.label: Bushahr
memes.description: 'A hill state in the Himalayan region, situated along the route taken by Russian spies as they travel eastward to avoid detection. (013-024)'

memes.label: Calcutta
memes.description: 'The capital of British India and terminus of the Grand Trunk Road, home to Calcutta University and a centre of Anglo-Indian education and society. (003-046)'

memes.label: "Caste"
memes.description: 'The hereditary social hierarchy of Hindu India that structures the society Kim navigates, influencing identity, occupation, and social interaction throughout the novel.'

memes.label: Chini
memes.description: 'A Himalayan valley on the route of the Russian agents in *Kim*, where Hurree Babu plans to intercept them as part of the Great Game.'

memes.label: "Chitor"
memes.description: 'An ancient fortified city in Rajasthan where the spy E23 hides from his pursuers and buries a secret letter during the Great Game narrative.'

memes.label: Chota Lal
memes.description: 'Chota Lal is a young Hindu boy who plays with Kim on the Zam-Zammah cannon at the beginning of the novel, representing the multi-ethnic world of Lahore street children (001-008).'

memes.label: Church of England in Kim
memes.description: 'The presence and role of the Church of England (Anglican Church) as depicted in Rudyard Kipling''s *Kim*, primarily embodied by the Reverend Arthur Bennett, chaplain of the Mavericks regiment.'

memes.label: "Colonel Creighton"
memes.description: 'The British officer who oversees the intelligence network of the Great Game in *Kim*, arranging Kim''s education and deployment as a spy while maintaining the respectable facade of an ethnologist and administrator.'

memes.label: "Confrontation with the Russian Spies"
memes.description: 'In the Himalayan hills, two foreign spies — one Russian, one French — confront Kim and the lama; the Russian tears the lama''s sacred diagram and strikes the old man, triggering a violent struggle.'

memes.label: Delhi
memes.description: 'The historic Mughal capital and a hub of the Great Game, Delhi appears throughout the novel as a destination, a threat, and a node in the intelligence network.'

memes.label: Doon
memes.description: 'The Doon is a half-tropical valley lying between the Siwalik Hills and the higher Himalayan ranges, serving as the transition zone between the plains and the mountains. (013-007)'

memes.label: Dora Pass
memes.description: 'A mountain pass on the North-West Frontier through which Mahbub Ali carries a critical intelligence report in Chapter 1 of *Kim*.'

memes.label: Dupont Sahib
memes.description: 'A European hunter in the Ladakh region, mentioned in passing as having missed a shot on a wild ibex — a minor figure whose name appears in the Russian agents'' cover story. (013-129)'

memes.label: E23
memes.description: 'A spy operative in the Great Game, code-named E23, a Mahratta agent trained by Lurgan Sahib whom Kim saves from capture on a train (Chapters 11-12).'

memes.label: "E.23 the Mahratta"
memes.description: "A British Indian intelligence agent codenamed E.23, trained by Lurgan Sahib, whom Kim rescues and disguises on a train journey before helping him evade capture at Delhi station. (011-126)"

memes.label: Ethnological Survey
memes.description: 'Colonel Creighton''s official department, a cover for British intelligence operations in the Great Game, conducting ethnographic research as a front for espionage activities across northern India.'

memes.description: 'Template validation page (event) — placeholder, pending removal or repurposing.'

memes.label: "Pilot event 9"
memes.description: 'Template validation page (event) — placeholder, pending removal or repurposing.'

memes.label: "Father Victor"
memes.description: 'The Catholic chaplain of the Mavericks regiment who identifies Kim as a white boy and sets in motion his enrollment at St. Xavier''s School, representing the religious arm of British colonial presence in India.'

memes.label: Ferozepore
memes.description: 'A town in the Punjab where Kimball O''Hara Sr. worked as a gang-foreman on the railway line, and where Kim''s mother died of cholera.'

memes.label: Fook Shing
memes.description: 'A Chinese bootmaker in the Lahore bazar whose wrinkled, yellow face the Teshoo Lama reminded Kim of when they first met.'

memes.label: Fostum Sahib
memes.description: 'A British hunter and sahib familiar to the hillfolk of the Himalayas, employer of local shikarris (trackers). (013-127)'

memes.label: Gugger River
memes.description: 'A river in the Punjab plains near Kalka where Kim nearly drowns during the horse-trading journey to Simla with Mahbub Ali in Chapter 8 of *Kim*.'

memes.label: Gunga
memes.description: 'The Ganges River as it appears in the lama''''s spiritual quest — the holy river that all Indians know but which the lama distinguishes from the River of the Arrow he seeks. (002-075)'

memes.label: Hanlé
memes.description: 'A settlement on the Indus River route through the Himalayas, mentioned by Hurree Babu as a known waypoint on the road through Ladakh. (013-024)'

memes.label: Hilás
memes.description: 'A hill state ruled by a Rajah with guns, one of a pair of principalities (with Bunár) that receive Russian agents and serve as intermediaries in the Great Game''s Himalayan frontier politics. (012-145)'

memes.label: "Huneefa"
memes.description: 'The blind woman of low caste who applies the final disguise to Kim before his mission into the hills, using dyes and charms to transform his appearance — a figure at the margins of respectability whose knowledge serves the Great Game.'

memes.label: "Hurree Babu"
memes.description: 'The Bengali intelligence agent whose comic facade of a babbling, cowardly clerk masks one of the most skilled operatives in the Great Game, embodying the novel''s theme of hidden depths beneath deceptive surfaces.'

memes.label: "Identity and Disguise"
memes.description: 'The central theme of fluid identity in *Kim*, where the protagonist''s ability to change his appearance and persona — passing as native or European at will — is both his greatest gift and his deepest existential challenge.'

memes.label: "Illusion and Reality"
memes.description: 'The thematic tension between appearance and truth that runs through *Kim*, linking the lama''s Buddhist teaching that all worldly things are illusion with the spy''s craft of deception and disguise.'

memes.label: Jadoo-Gher
memes.description: 'The "Magic House" — the Masonic Lodge in Lahore where Kimball O''Hara Sr. obtained the papers that would determine his son''s destiny. Kim and his father believed these documents held great magical power.'

memes.label: Jakko
memes.description: 'The deodar-crowned mountain above Simla where Lurgan Sahib''''s house is located, a setting for Kim''''s spy training and the cool retreat of British colonial society.'

memes.label: Jang-i-Lat Sahib
memes.description: 'The Commander-in-Chief of the British Indian Army, a guest at Creighton Sahib''''s dinner in Umballa where Kim overhears plans for a punitive expedition. (002-167)'

memes.label: "Journey on the Grand Trunk Road"
memes.description: 'Kim and the lama travel the Grand Trunk Road from Lahore toward Umballa, meeting a diverse procession of Indian life — the journey that binds master and disciple.'
aliases: Kim and the lama travel the GT Road

memes.label: Jullundur
memes.description: 'A district and city in the Punjab frequently mentioned in *Kim* — the homeland of the Jat farmer who travels with Kim, and a reference point for the rich agricultural character of the region.'

memes.label: Kabir
memes.description: 'A 15th-century Indian mystic poet and saint whose verses serve as epigraphs for several chapters of Kim, framing the spiritual themes of the narrative.'

memes.label: Kalka
memes.description: 'The railway terminus and road-head at the foot of the Simla hills, where travellers in *Kim* transfer from train to tonga for the mountain ascent to the summer capital.'

memes.label: "Kangra"
memes.description: "A hill district in present-day Himachal Pradesh, mentioned alongside Kulu as the homeland of the Kulu woman's hillfolk escort, known for its cool climate, pine forests, and mountain landscape. (004-053)"

memes.label: Kashmir
memes.description: 'A Himalayan region referenced in *Kim* through its earthquake, its saffron, the Kashmiri people, and the busy Kashmir Serai in Lahore.'

memes.label: Kedarnath
memes.description: 'A towering Himalayan peak, described alongside Badrinath as the "kings of the wilderness," visible from the high passes on the route through the mountains. (013-009)'

memes.label: "Kim Meets the Lama"
memes.description: 'The chance encounter at the Zam-Zammah cannon outside the Lahore Museum where Kim meets Teshoo Lama, setting the entire narrative in motion.'

memes.label: "Kimball O'Hara"
memes.description: 'The orphaned Irish boy raised in the Lahore bazaar who becomes a spy in the Great Game while serving as the disciple of a Tibetan lama.'
aliases: Little Friend of All the World

relations.label: Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: Kimball O'Hara Sr.
memes.description: 'Kimball O''Hara Sr. was a young colour-sergeant of the Mavericks regiment and Kim''s father, whose prophecy of the Red Bull shaped Kim''s destiny (001-003).'

memes.label: "Kim's Capture by the Mavericks"
memes.description: 'Kim wanders into the Mavericks regimental camp, where Father Victor and Bennett discover his identity through the amulet — the prophecy of the Red Bull is fulfilled.'

memes.label: "Kim's Disguise Transformation"
memes.description: 'At Huneefa''s house in Lucknow, Kim undergoes his first professional disguise: a blind sorceress dyes his skin dark and transforms him from a Sahib schoolboy into a low-caste Hindu boy, marking his transition from trainee to field operative in the Great Game.'

memes.label: "Kim's Document Theft"
memes.description: 'In the chaos following the Russian''s assault on the lama, Kim and Hurree Babu recover the foreign spies'' maps, documents, and intelligence — a pivotal counter-intelligence operation in the Great Game.'

memes.label: "Kim's First Spy Mission at Quetta"
memes.description: 'While on monsoon leave in Quetta, Kim undertakes his first independent intelligence operation — copying the commissariat sergeant''s ledger to gather military supply information for the Great Game.'

memes.label: "Kim's Great Game Recruitment"
memes.description: 'Colonel Creighton formally decides to train Kim as a spy operative for the Great Game, setting him on the path from street boy to intelligence agent.'

memes.label: "Kim's Identity Crisis"
memes.description: 'A recurring psychological crisis in which Kim confronts the question "Who is Kim?" — a struggle between his Irish heredity, Indian upbringing, spy training, and devotion to the lama, surfacing three times across the novel.'
aliases: I am Kim and what is Kim

memes.label: "Kim's Recovery and Return"
memes.description: 'After his spy mission against the Russian agents, Kim collapses from exhaustion and is nursed back to health by the Kulu woman and the lama, emerging from his fevered dreams with a new understanding of his identity and purpose.'
aliases: Kim nursed by the Kulu woman

memes.label: "Kim's Schooling at St. Xavier's"
memes.description: 'Kim is sent to St. Xavier''s School in Lucknow for formal education, transforming from a street boy into a Sahib — a crucial step in his training for the Great Game.'

memes.label: Kotgarh
memes.description: 'A settlement in the Himalayan hills known for its Christian mission station and European colony, located within reach of Shamlegh and the Chini valley. (014-069)'

memes.label: Kulu
memes.description: 'A remote Himalayan region and the homeland of the Kulu woman, representing the northern threshold of the lama''s journey from Tibet into India.'

memes.label: "Ladakh"
memes.description: 'A high-altitude region in the northern Himalayas, referenced in the Great Game as a route for spies and traders traversing between India and Tibet.'

memes.label: Lahore
memes.description: 'The walled city in Punjab where *Kim* opens and Kimball O''Hara grows up as a street boy, a crossroads of Indian, British, and Afghan cultures.'

memes.label: Leh
memes.description: 'A high Himalayan town on the trade routes between India and Tibet, mentioned throughout *Kim* as a waypoint for spies, traders, and pilgrims crossing the northern passes.'

memes.label: "Loyalty and Betrayal"
memes.description: 'The ethical tension between loyalty and betrayal that underlies all relationships in *Kim*, from Kim''s dual allegiance to the lama and the Great Game, to the precarious trust that holds the intelligence network together.'

memes.label: Lucknow
memes.description: 'The city in Oudh where St Xavier''s School is located, where Kim receives his formal education for three years.'

memes.label: "Lung-Cho"
memes.description: "A Tibetan monastery whose Abbot provided the Teshoo Lama with the drawing of the Wheel of Life that launched his pilgrimage, and whose authority is respected by the Kulu woman. (001-050)"

memes.label: "Lurgan Sahib"
memes.description: 'The mysterious Simla-based spymaster who trains Kim in observation, memory, and disguise — the essential skills of the Great Game.'

memes.label: Lutuf Ullah
memes.description: 'Lutuf Ullah is Mahbub Ali''s horse-trading partner, a tall man with a broken nose who operates between Delhi and the northern frontier (001-162).'

memes.label: "Mahbub Ali"
memes.description: 'The Pathan horse-trader who serves as a British intelligence agent and Kim''s first mentor in the Great Game, recruiting him into the network of spies operating across India.'

memes.label: Mahbub Ali's Ambush and Pedigree Theft
memes.description: 'Enemy agents drug Mahbub Ali in Lahore and steal the secret pedigree of the white stallion, triggering Kim''s first covert mission to Umballa (001-163).'

relations.label: The Babu's Escape from the Russian Spies
Kim's First Spy Mission at Quetta

memes.label: "Mahbub Ali's Message"
memes.description: 'Kim carries a secret message from Mahbub Ali to Colonel Creighton at Umballa, marking his first act as a spy and entry into the Great Game.'
aliases: Kim delivers Mahbub's message at Umballa

memes.label: Mian Mir
memes.description: 'A railway station near Lahore where Kim buys a ticket for himself and the lama, and where the guard nearly ejects them for a ticketing irregularity. (002-014)'

memes.label: Motee Bazar
memes.description: 'A bustling market in Lahore through which Kim navigated daily life. It is where the Teshoo Lama first appeared, shuffling round the corner into Kim''s world.'

memes.label: "Mr. Bennett"
memes.description: 'The Anglican chaplain of the Mavericks who, alongside Father Victor, identifies Kim as a white boy — representing the Protestant establishment of colonial India.'

memes.label: Murasla
memes.description: 'A King''s letter—a sealed, gilded, and illuminated diplomatic document carried by the Russian spies, containing correspondence of political and intelligence significance. (013-088)'

memes.label: Mussoorie
memes.description: 'A Himalayan hill station in *Kim* that marks the transition from the plains to the mountains, appearing as a waypoint on the journey northward and a destination for British society in the hills.'

memes.label: Nahan
memes.description: 'A princely state in the Himalayan foothills whose Rajah mistook Kim and the lama for Europeans. (015-004)'

memes.label: Nikal Seyn
memes.description: 'John Nicholson (1822–1857), a British general remembered in Punjabi folk song as "Nikal Seyn," who died in the storming of Delhi during the Indian Rebellion. (003-184)'

memes.label: Nila Ram
memes.description: 'A timber-yard owner in Lahore beyond the Punjab High Court, where Kim stored his Hindu disguise under some baulks of deodar wood.'

memes.label: Nilang Pass
memes.description: 'A high Himalayan pass crossed by the lama and Kim in the final chapter of *Kim*, part of the arduous mountain journey toward the River of the Arrow.'

memes.label: Ooryas
memes.description: 'Soldiers from the Odisha (Orissa) region of eastern India, serving in the British Indian Army alongside hillmen and other regional recruits in the Mavericks regimental camp.'

memes.label: "Oudh"
memes.description: "A historical region in North India (present-day Uttar Pradesh), referenced in the novel as a source of landholders, a region associated with the slave trade, and connected to the social geography of the Mavericks regiment. (004-087)"

memes.label: Patiala
memes.description: 'A princely state in Punjab where Kim attended a great feast; a fireworks explosion killed eleven men, including the firework-maker, and blew Kim across a tent. (007-133)'

memes.label: "Pilot Person 6"
memes.description: 'Template validation page (person) — placeholder, pending removal or repurposing.'

memes.label: "Pilot Person 7"
memes.description: 'Template validation page (person) — placeholder, pending removal or repurposing.'

memes.label: Peshawur
memes.description: 'A frontier city on the North-West Border of British India, a key node in the Great Game spy network and arms smuggling routes. (001-202)'

memes.label: "Pilgrimage"
memes.description: 'The spiritual practice of journeying to sacred sites that structures the physical movement of *Kim*, as the lama''s pilgrimage to find the River of the Arrow becomes the narrative framework for Kim''s double education in spiritual devotion and worldly espionage.'

memes.label: Pirzai Kotal
memes.description: 'A mountain pass on the North-West Frontier where the Ludhiana Sikhs and Dogra companies fought against eight Afridi standards in a border skirmish.'

memes.description: 'Template validation page (place) — placeholder, pending removal or repurposing.'

memes.label: Prayag
memes.description: 'The ancient pilgrimage city at the confluence of the Ganges and Yamuna rivers, known to Hindus as Prayag and to the British as Allahabad. (003-045)'

memes.label: Punjab
memes.description: 'The primary geographical setting of *Kim*, the fertile north-Indian province where the novel''s action begins and to which its characters repeatedly refer as home.'
aliases: the Land of the Five Rivers

memes.label: Quetta
memes.description: 'A strategic border city near the Afghan frontier, a hub for arms smuggling and intelligence operations in the Great Game, where Kim conducts his first spy mission as a scullion.'

relations.label: Kim's First Spy Mission at Quetta

memes.label: Rampur
memes.description: 'A Himalayan princely state in *Kim* whose Rajah provides Hurree Babu with his cover identity, allowing him to approach the Russian agents undetected during the Great Game pursuit.'

memes.label: Reverend Arthur Bennett
memes.description: 'The Church of England Chaplain of the Mavericks regiment who discovers Kim in the camp and, together with Father Victor, identifies him as Kimball O''Hara''s son.'

memes.label: Saharunpore
memes.description: 'A small fruit-growing town on the Grand Trunk Road where the lama recuperates and a key waypoint on Kim and the lama''s journey across northern India.'

memes.label: Sanawar
memes.description: 'A hill station and the site of the Lawrence Military Asylum, where Kim was initially intended to be sent for schooling before the plan changed to St. Xavier''s in Lucknow. (005-197)'

memes.label: "Secrecy and Surveillance"
memes.description: 'The twin practices of concealment and observation that define the operations of the Great Game, where knowledge is power and being watched without knowing it is the ultimate vulnerability.'

memes.label: Shamlegh
memes.description: 'A remote Himalayan village and espionage waypoint in Chapters 13–14 of *Kim*, where Kim outmaneuvers Russian agents and secures the lama''s safety.'

memes.label: Shamlegh-midden
memes.description: 'A deep, inaccessible gorge below the village of Shamlegh, used by the hillfolk as a dumping ground where no man has ever set foot. (013-136)'

memes.label: Sikandar Khan
memes.description: 'A horse trader and kafila (caravan) head who appears in Chapters 1 and 8 of the novel. (001-218)'

memes.label: Simla
memes.description: 'The British summer capital in the Himalayas, centre of intelligence operations in *Kim* where Colonel Creighton and Lurgan Sahib direct the Great Game.'

memes.label: Siwaliks
memes.description: 'The Siwalik Hills, the outermost range of the Himalayan foothills, forming a boundary between the Indian plains and the higher mountains. (013-007)'

memes.label: Somna Road
memes.description: 'A railway station on the line through the Punjab where, in Chapter 11 of *Kim*, the spy E.23 boards the train and is recognised by Kim.'

memes.label: Spiti
memes.description: 'A high Himalayan region in *Kim* whose men appear as coolies and whose landscape forms part of the lama and Kim''s journey northward through the mountains.'

memes.label: Srinagar
memes.description: 'The capital city of Kashmir, site of a great earthquake on the night of Kim''''s birth that served as a historical marker for dating his age. (002-181)'

memes.label: "St. Xavier's School"
memes.description: 'The British-style boarding school in Lucknow where Kim is sent to receive a European education, representing the colonial attempt to civilize and control while ironically providing Kim with skills essential to his work in the Great Game.'

memes.label: Strickland Sahib
memes.description: 'A British District Superintendent of Police and operative in the Great Game who helps E23 escape capture at Delhi station (Chapter 12).'

memes.label: Such-zen
memes.description: 'The lama''s home monastery in Tibet, opposite the Painted Rocks, where he served as Abbot before beginning his pilgrimage to India. (001-053)'

memes.label: Taksali Gate
memes.description: 'A gate in the old city of Lahore, known in *Kim* as a gathering place for faquirs and a site of the street wisdom that shaped Kim''s childhood.'

memes.label: "Teacher and Student"
memes.description: 'The guru-chela (teacher-student) relationship that structures Kim''s education in both spiritual wisdom and worldly craft, embodied in his dual apprenticeship to the Teshoo Lama and the spymasters of the Great Game.'

memes.label: "Teshoo Lama"
memes.description: 'The Tibetan Buddhist monk whose pilgrimage to find the River of the Arrow forms the spiritual backbone of *Kim*, serving as Kim''s guru and moral compass.'

memes.label: "The Babu"
memes.description: 'A minor character in *Kim* representing the class of English-educated Bengali clerks who served the British administration.'

memes.label: "The Babu's Escape from the Russian Spies"
memes.description: 'Hurree Chunder Mookerjee, the Bengali Babu, ingratiates himself with two foreign spies in the Himalayan hills, upsets their document-carrying kilta, feeds them drink, and — under cover of buffoonery — collects the intelligence that serves the Great Game.'

memes.label: "The Chain Men"
memes.description: 'The covert intelligence network of British India that operates as the backbone of the Great Game, connecting agents across the subcontinent through a chain of informants and handlers.'

memes.label: "The Curator"
memes.description: 'The English scholar-curator of the Lahore Museum who welcomes the Teshoo Lama and recognizes the depth of his Buddhist learning, representing the possibility of genuine cross-cultural understanding within the colonial framework.'

relations.label: The Lama Visits the Wonder House

memes.label: "The Grand Trunk Road"
memes.description: 'The historic highway stretching across northern India that serves as the primary setting for much of *Kim* and symbolizes the diverse, interconnected life of British India.'

memes.label: "The Great Game"
memes.description: 'The covert political and intelligence conflict between Britain and Russia in Central Asia during the 19th century, which serves as the central political framework of Rudyard Kipling''s novel *Kim*.'

memes.label: "The Jewel Game at Lurgan's"
memes.description: 'Lurgan Sahib tests Kim with the tray of jewels memory game — a training exercise designed to sharpen observation and recall, in which Kim''s remarkable performance proves his suitability for intelligence work.'

memes.label: The Kamboh
memes.description: 'A Punjabi farmer of the Kamboh caste from Jullundur who becomes Kim and the lama''''s traveling companion in Chapter 11, providing practical wisdom, cooking skills, and a child whose life Kim saves.'

memes.label: "The Kulu Woman"
memes.description: 'The wealthy widow from Kulu who travels with Kim and the lama along the Grand Trunk Road, providing material support and forming one of the few significant female presences in the novel.'

memes.label: "The Lama Visits the Wonder House"
memes.description: 'The Teshoo Lama enters the Lahore Museum — the Wonder House — where the British Curator shows him Buddhist artifacts and confirms the existence of the River of the Arrow, setting the lama''s pilgrimage in motion.'

memes.label: "The Lama's Enlightenment"
memes.description: 'After years of pilgrimage across India, the lama at last finds the River of the Arrow — the stream that washes away the ''stain of sin'' — achieving the spiritual liberation that has driven him since the novel''s opening pages.'
aliases: the River of the Arrow found

memes.label: "The Lama's First Train Journey"
memes.description: 'The Teshoo Lama boards a train for the first time at Lahore station, experiencing modern technology with wonder and fear — a moment of cultural collision that marks the beginning of his journey across India.'

memes.label: "The Lama's Search Begins"
memes.description: 'The lama explains his spiritual Search for the River of the Arrow to Kim and fellow travelers, marking the beginning of his sacred journey that defines the novel''s spiritual arc (002-114).'
aliases: the Search for the River of the Arrow

memes.label: The Ludhiana Sikhs
memes.description: 'A Sikh regiment of the British Indian Army whose soldiers Kim and the lama encounter on the train to Umballa. The regiment fought at the Pirzai Kotal border action.'

memes.label: "The Mavericks"
memes.description: 'The fictional Irish regiment of the British Army in which Kimball O''Hara Sr. served as a colour-sergeant, and which later identifies Kim as one of its own.'

relations.label: Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: The Mutiny
memes.description: 'The Indian Rebellion of 1857, referred to as "the Mutiny" or "the Black Year," a cataclysmic event that shapes the memory and identity of the old soldiers Kim encounters. (003-064)'

memes.label: "The Old Soldier"
memes.description: 'The retired Indian soldier who befriends the lama and Kim on the Grand Trunk Road, representing the generation of Indians who served the British and carried their stories.'

memes.label: "The Old Soldier's Mutiny Tale"
memes.description: 'A retired Risaldar-major recounts his experiences in the 1857 Indian Mutiny to Kim and the lama, weaving personal memory into a larger narrative of loyalty and war.'

memes.label: "The Prophecy of the Red Bull"
memes.description: 'Kim''s father prophesied that a Red Bull on a green field — the crest of the Mavericks regiment — would come for his son; the prophecy is fulfilled when Kim stumbles into the regiment''s camp near Umballa.'
aliases: the Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: The Queen's Stone
memes.description: 'A secret communication point at Chitor used by Great Game operatives as a dead drop for hiding and retrieving documents (Chapters 11-12).'

memes.label: "The Red Bull Prophecy"
memes.description: 'The prophecy left by Kim''s father that his son would one day find a Red Bull on a green field, which is fulfilled when Kim encounters the Mavericks regimental flag — a moment that sets his dual identity as Sahib and native into motion.'
aliases: Red Bull on a green field

memes.label: "The River of the Arrow"
memes.description: 'The mythical river that the lama seeks throughout *Kim*, whose waters are said to wash away sin and free the soul from the Wheel of Life.'

memes.label: "The Russian Agents"
memes.description: "Two foreign intelligence agents — one Russian, one French — who operate in the Himalayan hills under cover of a hunting expedition, serving as the antagonists in the novel's final section. Their confrontation with Kim and the lama precipitates the climactic events. (012-149)"

memes.label: 'The Russian Spies Enter the Hills'
memes.description: 'Kim and the lama first learn of two foreign agents — one Russian, one French — operating in the Himalayan region, setting up the confrontation of Chapters 13-14 (012-149).'
aliases: Russian and French spies in the hills

memes.label: "The Search for the River"
memes.description: 'The lama''s climactic search for the mythical River of the Arrow in the Himalayas, which resolves both his spiritual quest and the novel''s espionage plot.'

memes.label: The Serai
memes.description: 'The travellers'' rest-house in Lahore where Mahbub Ali keeps his quarters and Kim begins his unwitting apprenticeship in the Great Game.'

memes.label: "The Survey of India"
memes.description: 'The British colonial mapping institution serving dual purposes — legitimate scientific survey and cover for intelligence gathering in the Great Game.'

memes.label: "The Wheel of Life"
memes.description: 'The Buddhist concept of cyclical existence (samsara) that the lama seeks to escape through enlightenment, central to the spiritual dimension of Kipling''s *Kim*.'

memes.label: "The Woman of Shamlegh's Rescue"
memes.description: 'After the Russian''s assault on the lama, the Woman of Shamlegh provides food, shelter, and intelligence to Kim and the injured lama at Shamlegh-under-the-Snow.'

memes.label: The Wonder House
memes.description: 'The Lahore Museum, called the Wonder House by locals, where Kim first encounters the Teshoo Lama and the narrative of the novel is set in motion.'

memes.label: Tibet
memes.description: 'The lama''s distant Himalayan homeland, a presence throughout *Kim* that shapes the novel''s spiritual geography and drives the Search for the River of the Arrow.'

memes.label: "Tirthankars' Temple"
memes.description: "A Jain temple about a mile outside Benares where the Teshoo Lama establishes his base during Kim''s schooling, using it as a mailing address, a place of spiritual debate, and a fixed point in his wandering search for the River."

memes.label: "Training with Lurgan Sahib"
memes.description: "Kim undergoes intensive spy training at Lurgan Sahib's shop in Simla — learning memory games, jewel identification, and disguise — preparing him for the Great Game."

memes.label: "Umballa"
memes.description: 'The Punjab railway junction city that serves as a critical node in the Great Game, where Colonel Creighton is stationed and the Mavericks regiment camps en route to the front.'

memes.label: Yankling Sahib
memes.description: 'A British hunter and sahib in the Himalayan hills, employer of hillmen shikarris (trackers), known among the hillfolk as a "merry-minded man." (013-115)'

memes.label: Zam-Zammah
memes.description: 'The massive green-bronze cannon outside the Lahore Museum upon which Kim is first introduced, a children''s perch that symbolises contested colonial authority.'

memes.label: Ziglaur
memes.description: 'A village in the Chini valley region of the Himalayas, serving as a meeting point and resting place for travelers on the hill roads. (013-033)'
